title: Second cancer-site analysis pack
context: BACKLOG: Only after M6 is complete and the maintenance rotation is in place. The parameterized analysis module built for the exemplar site makes adding a second cancer site a configuration exercise rather than a rewrite, but the full methods + framing review gate applies to each new site. Site selection follows the same scored criteria used in disparities-analysis-site-004.
objective: Select, acquire, analyze, and deliver a complete disparity analysis pack for a second cancer site using the parameterized acquisition and analysis modules built in M1-M3. Apply the same methods standard, framing policy, suppression rules, and review gates as the exemplar site. Produce the full output set: suppressed data cube, age-standardized rates + CIs + trends, absolute + relative disparity + index of disparity, technical brief, and health-equity narrative report — all methods-reviewed and framing-reviewed.
acceptanceCriteria: Second cancer site is selected using the same scored criteria as disparities-analysis-site-004 (open-data feasibility, actionability, reviewer availability, partner fit)
Acquisition module is run for the second site using the parameterized configuration; new Source provenance records created
Suppressed canonical data cube is built and the feasibility kill-gate is passed (or a pivot decision recorded) for the second site
Age-standardized rates, CIs, stability flags, joinpoint trends, and absolute + relative disparity (+ index of disparity, HD*Calc-validated) are computed for the second site
Technical disparity brief and health-equity narrative report are produced with evidence-tiered framing, explicit limitations, and all claims sourced
Full methods + framing review gate applies: methods reviewer and framing reviewer sign-off recorded for the second site's outputs
Reproducibility harness extended to cover the second site; all figures reproduced in CI
output: A complete analysis pack for a second cancer site: suppressed data cube (dataset artifact), rates + trends code, disparity measures, technical brief, and narrative report — all methods-reviewed and framing-reviewed.
